title: Basis of Design Threatened by Competitors
openingLine: Hey, I just issued the addendum on the Crestview project. I know Wenger was basis of design on the instrument storage but the casework contractor pushed back hard and we had to approve two competitors as equals. I figured I'd give you a heads up.
customerContext: You spec'd Wenger as basis of design for instrument storage but it got lumped into the general casework package. The casework GC complained about pricing and pushed for substitutions. You approved two competitor cabinet manufacturers as equals — you didn't really evaluate them, you just trusted the contractor's word that they were comparable. You're feeling a little guilty about it but you also don't want to fight the contractor over $30K of cabinets.

hiddenInfo.reveal: Casework contractor said the alternates were equals and saved budget. I didn't do a deep comparison.
I don't have a current instrument list — just specified standard cabinet sizes from Wenger's catalog.
The music director was at the early design meetings but wasn't consulted on the addendum.
District has Wenger storage in two other buildings. They've been happy with it.
Bid is in three weeks. Casework is its own bid line in the general construction package.
CM is Hanlon Builders. They have their own preferred casework subs.
$32M new high school. Music storage scope is roughly $180K.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked architect why the competitors were approved as equals
Asked whether the music director / owner was consulted
Asked about prior district Wenger purchases
Asked whether an instrument list exists to validate the cabinets

Solution Framing.behavior: Offered cabinet samples to demonstrate differentiation
Reinforced Wenger value vs. competitors (warranty, materials, testing)
Offered a detailed cabinet layout to support the spec

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Asked CM/GC if music casework can be a separate bid package
Engaged owner directly to confirm they want the Wenger solution
Reviewed documents for other Wenger product opportunities on the project

BANT.behavior: Aligned on pricing strategy with internal team

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Set tasks for pre-bid and post-bid follow-through

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Alerted owner directly about the addendum impact
